title: thelypteris endertii
intro: Thelypteris endertii, also known as the Thai maidenhair, is a species of fern native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its delicate and lacy fronds. Traditionally, Thelypteris endertii is used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, Thelypteris endertii is often used in teas, infusions, and poultices to harness its medicinal properties.
